calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker and Andrew Davies for and with Michael McCowan (Administrator/Petitioner).

Mr. McCowan testified under oath that he has viewed the supplemental declaration, that he verifies that $230,474.87 were transferred out of the Zinn Law Firm Attorney Client trust account and deposited into Citi National Bank, and that the statements are true and correct. The Court granted the Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. (subject to review of the order). Mr. Boessenecker struck language in page 3, lines 5 and 6 of the proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Continued To May-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker for Michael McCowan (Administrator).
Andrew Davies and Carter Zinn litigation Counsel for Michael McGowan.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that the Decedent had an injury and committed suicide by the time the settlement was done, that the money from the lawsuit came to the Administrator and went into the Attorney's trust account, that the only asset of the estate was the litigation and that he did not submit any time records for any statutory fees. The Court stated that it is not going to allow $18,000.00 in fees when everything that was done was for the litigation, and that it will do both statutory and extraordinary fees. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the returns that are required to be filed will be filed, but there is no income so no fiduciary returns are needed. Mr. Zinn stated that Mr. McCowan is going to get $40,000.00, spoke of the fee agreement, stated that the gross estate share was $93,000.00 in the first case, that Medicare ate all of it, spoke of how the value of the medical malpractice case plummeted upon Mr. Zeller's death, the cost of having witnesses, the cost of litigation, the level of work performed by the firm, the number of hours he spent working the case, etc. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he will file a declaration regarding his fees/time within the next week. The Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation
Off Calendar Mar-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Continued To Mar-28-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-17-2018 Continued To Feb-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Davies litigation Counsel for Michael McCowan.

Mr. Davies stated that he will be filing a petition for approval of settlement of the litigation matter within a week, that the settlement will be the only asset of the Estate, that it will be unopposed by the Department of Health Care Services and Mr. Urbatsch, and that the City and County of San Francisco still has not approved the settlement. Status on close of estate is continued to February 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Counsel that an inventory and appraisal, and either a petition for approval of report on status of administration or a petition for final distribution needs to be filed by February 8, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
